channel.title: Gardening on Basil 101
channel.description: Recent content in Gardening on Basil 101

item.title: Nature Moves Fast: Three-Day Basil Success
item.description:
Just three days ago, I planted basil seeds, and today they are already showing off. I noticed the tiny “elbows” of the sprouts pushing through the soil, followed quickly by the first set of miniature leaves.

Normally, the books say to expect germination between five to fourteen days, so seeing this progress in just seventy-two hours feels like a small miracle. It’s a reminder that nature often has its own schedule regardless of what the guides suggest. Watching these tiny greens emerge so far ahead of schedule is the perfect boost for a Thursday afternoon. There is something deeply satisfying about documenting this kind of quiet, rapid growth in the soil.

item.title: From Seed to Sprout: The Basil Journey Begins
item.description:
Welcome to the very first entry of my growth journal! On April 11th, I officially kicked off this season by seeding six trays of basil. I decided to start this blog specifically to document their journey from tiny black specks to lush, aromatic bushes.

Today is April 15th, and while it’s only been four days, the anticipation is building. The weather has been absolutely perfect lately, with temperatures hovering between 22°C and 28°C. Basil is a true sun-lover, and this warm range is the “sweet spot” for germination.
